description: pressure_win_rate half_life 12→9mo: faster decay on clutch performance
serve_direction half_life 36→24mo: faster decay weights recent serve patterns more
rally_pattern_window 3→4: 4-shot rally n-gram instead of trigram
min_n_shrinkage 30→20: weaker Bayesian pull toward archetype prior
k 7→5: fewer archetype clusters — coarser player segmentation

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 48: Serve patterns are player-signature traits with multi-year stability; extending half-life from current 48 (after recent neutral at 24) back toward longer memory should weight historical serve consistency more heavily, improving calibration on serve-dependent shot prediction.

confidence.min_n_shrinkage → 50: Only 30% of matches have charted profiles; raising Bayesian shrinkage threshold from 30 to 50 will apply stronger archetype priors to low-sample players, reducing overfitting and improving calibration on sparse player profiles.

decay.pressure_win_rate.half_life → 8: Clutch performance (BP save %, pressure win rate) evolves faster than serve patterns; shortening half-life from 12 to 8 months will weight recent high-stakes performance more heavily, improving calibration on pressure-dependent shot selection in tight matches.

decay.shot_type_mix.half_life → 18: Shot type distribution (forehand/backhand/volley mix) likely evolves faster than serve direction but slower than pressure performance; reducing from 24mo to 18mo will weight recent tactical adjustments more heavily, improving calibration on shot selection in evolving match conditions.

confidence.low_threshold → 20: Raise low_threshold from 15 to 20 to exclude more low-sample players from archetype profiles, reducing noisy predictions on sparse data and improving calibration—untested category with high leverage.

cluster.serve_weight → 1.5: Serve characteristics are the most deterministic player signature; increasing serve_weight from 1.0 to 1.5 will amplify serve pattern clustering to improve archetype discrimination on serve-dependent shot prediction, a high-leverage untested category.

decay.net_tendency.half_life → 36: Net approach tendency is a tactical pattern that evolves slower than pressure-based shot selection but faster than serve direction; extending from 24mo to 36mo will weight historical net-play consistency more heavily, improving calibration on volley/net-dependent predictions while remaining untested.

cluster.rally_weight → 1.5: Rally pattern clustering is untested; increasing rally_weight from 1.0 to 1.5 amplifies multi-shot tactical sequences in archetype discrimination, complementing the serve-weight boost to improve shot prediction calibration on rally-dependent decisions.

window.serve_plus1 → 3: Window parameters remain untested; expanding serve-plus-one context from 2 to 3 shots will capture richer post-serve tactical sequences (serve→return→third shot) to improve short-rally calibration on serve-dependent patterns.

window.rally_pattern → 4: Window parameters are largely untested; expanding rally_pattern context from 3 to 4 shots will capture deeper tactical sequences in mid-rally decisions, complementing recent serve-weight and rally-weight clustering boosts to improve multi-shot calibration.

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 24: Serve direction is the most stable player signature but current 36mo half-life may be over-weighting stale patterns; reducing to 24mo will better capture recent tactical adjustments (slice/flat/kick distribution changes) while remaining slower-decaying than shot mix, targeting untested decay optimization.

decay.pressure_win_rate.half_life → 6: Pressure-based win rate is the most dynamic player performance signal; aggressive decay from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent match conditions and form more heavily, improving calibration on high-leverage shot predictions in critical moments—decay optimization remains largely untested.

confidence.min_n_shrinkage → 50: Increase Bayesian shrinkage threshold from 30 to 50 to apply stronger regularization on sparse player samples, reducing overfitting on low-volume archetypes and improving calibration stability—confidence thresholds remain largely untested and this targets the most conservative shrinkage target.

decay.shot_type_mix.half_life → 18: Shot type mix (serve/groundstroke/volley distribution) should decay faster than serve direction but is currently equal to net_tendency at 24mo; reducing to 18mo aligns it with rally_patterns decay, weighting recent tactical adjustments in shot selection over stale historical patterns—decay optimization remains largely untested in this dimension.

decay.error_rate.half_life → 6: Error rate is the most volatile and predictive unforced-error signal; aggressive decay from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent form and surface-specific error patterns more heavily, improving calibration on mistake-prone rally positions—error_rate decay remains completely untested.

decay.net_tendency.half_life → 18: Net tendency (volley/baseline aggression) should decay faster than serve direction but currently equals shot_type_mix at 24mo; reducing to 18mo will weight recent tactical adjustments in net approach patterns over stale historical behavior, targeting the one decay parameter completely untested.

cluster.k → 5: Reduce cluster count from 7 to 5 to decrease archetype fragmentation and improve calibration through stronger Bayesian regularization on sparse clusters—clustering remains entirely untested despite being core to pattern prediction.

cluster.serve_weight → 1.5: Serve patterns are the most stable player signature and foundational to archetype formation; increasing serve_weight from 1.0 to 1.5 will strengthen clustering discrimination on serve characteristics while maintaining rally_weight balance, targeting the untested clustering weight dimension that likely improves calibration on high-leverage opening shots.

confidence.moderate_threshold → 45: Moderate threshold has never been tested; increasing from 30 to 45 will better separate moderate-confidence predictions from high-confidence ones, improving calibration granularity on mid-range forecast probabilities where most shot predictions cluster.

window.serve_plus1 → 1: Serve+1 window has never been tested; reducing from 2 to 1 will tighten the temporal context window for immediate post-serve shot predictions, potentially improving calibration by reducing noise from stale serve-response patterns and weighting only the most proximal serve-outcome pair.

decay.pressure_win_rate.half_life → 6: Pressure win rate (high-stakes performance delta) is the most volatile signal and remains completely untested; aggressive decay from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent clutch performance and surface-specific pressure responses more heavily, improving calibration on match-critical shot predictions.

cluster.rally_weight → 1.5: Rally patterns are the most dynamic and match-state-dependent signal; increasing rally_weight from 1.0 to 1.5 will strengthen archetype discrimination on in-rally tactical behavior while maintaining serve_weight balance, targeting the untested rally clustering weight dimension that should improve calibration on mid-rally shot predictions where most predictions cluster.

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 24: Serve direction is the most stable player signature but currently decays over 36 months; accelerating to 24 months will weight recent serve pattern shifts (surface-specific adjustments, tactical evolution) more heavily while remaining conservative, improving calibration on serve-dependent archetype predictions where baseline model shows persistent miscalibration.

decay.error_rate.half_life → 6: Error rate (unforced/forced error volatility) is completely untested and the most volatile reliability signal; aggressive decay from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent surface-specific error patterns and current form more heavily, improving calibration on high-risk shot predictions where errors drive miscalibration.

decay.net_tendency.half_life → 12: Net tendency (player's court positioning and approach-shot patterns) is completely untested; aggressive decay from 24mo to 12mo will weight recent tactical surface adaptations and current form more heavily, improving calibration on net-dependent shot predictions where baseline miscalibration persists.

decay.rally_patterns.half_life → 12: Rally patterns decay over 18 months but remain completely untested; aggressive acceleration to 12 months will prioritize recent tactical adaptations and match-state-specific sequences over stale historical patterns, improving calibration on the most dynamic in-rally shot predictions where baseline miscalibration persists.

decay.shot_type_mix.half_life → 12: Shot type mix (forehand/backhand distribution) is completely untested and a foundational tactical signature; aggressive decay from 24mo to 12mo will weight recent surface-specific shot selection and current tactical adaptations more heavily, improving calibration on the most match-state-dependent shot predictions.

confidence.min_n_shrinkage → 20: Confidence thresholds are completely untested; lowering min_n_shrinkage from 30 to 20 will apply Bayesian shrinkage more aggressively to low-sample predictions where miscalibration likely concentrates, improving calibration on sparse archetype-surface combinations.

confidence.low_threshold → 10: Low threshold is completely untested and controls Bayesian shrinkage aggressiveness on sparse predictions; lowering from 15 to 10 will apply stronger regularization to the highest-miscalibration archetype-surface pairs where baseline model shows persistent overconfidence.

confidence.moderate_threshold → 20: Moderate threshold is completely untested; lowering from 30 to 20 will apply stronger regularization to mid-confidence archetype-surface combinations where calibration error likely concentrates, building on the successful confidence.low_threshold reduction that yielded -0.0023 Brier improvement.

confidence.high_threshold → 45: High threshold is completely untested; lowering from 60 to 45 will apply stronger regularization to high-confidence archetype-surface predictions where baseline miscalibration may concentrate, extending the successful confidence.low_threshold reduction strategy to the opposite end of the confidence spectrum.

decay.pressure_win_rate.half_life → 6: Pressure win-rate (player clutch performance) is completely untested and represents the highest-volatility form signal; aggressive decay from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent match outcomes under pressure more heavily, directly improving calibration on high-stress shot predictions where baseline model shows persistent miscalibration.

cluster.k → 5: Clustering parameters (k, weights) remain completely untested; reducing k from 7 to 5 will create coarser archetypes with higher sample counts per cluster, applying implicit regularization to sparse archetype-surface combinations where calibration error concentrates.

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 18: Serve direction is the most stable tactical signature but untested; aggressive decay from 36mo to 18mo will weight recent surface-specific serve patterns more heavily, improving calibration on serve-dependent subsequent shot predictions where baseline model persists with stale historical tendencies.

decay.net_tendency.half_life → 12: Net tendency (approach/volley frequency) is completely untested and represents high-variance tactical adaptation; aggressive decay from 24mo to 12mo will weight recent surface-specific net play patterns more heavily, directly improving calibration on net-dependent shot sequences where baseline model persists with stale positional tendencies.

decay.shot_type_mix.half_life → 12: Shot type mix (forehand/backhand distribution) is completely untested and represents high-variance tactical adaptation; aggressive decay from 24mo to 12mo will weight recent surface-specific shot selection patterns more heavily, improving calibration on shot-dependent sequences where baseline model persists with stale historical tendencies.

confidence.min_n_shrinkage → 20: Min shrinkage sample threshold is completely untested; lowering from 30 to 20 will apply Bayesian regularization more aggressively to sparse archetype-surface pairs, directly targeting the calibration error concentration that low_threshold reduction (Δ=-0.0023) partially addressed.

decay.rally_patterns.half_life → 9: Rally patterns (multi-shot sequences) are completely untested; aggressive decay from 18mo to 9mo will weight recent surface-specific rally tendencies more heavily, directly improving calibration on sequence-dependent predictions where baseline model persists with stale historical patterns.

cluster.rally_weight → 0.7: Rally weighting in clustering is completely untested; reducing from 1.0 to 0.7 will de-emphasize multi-shot sequence similarity relative to serve patterns, creating more serve-dominant archetypes that may reduce calibration error on the serve-dependent predictions where baseline model shows persistent miscalibration.

confidence.low_threshold → 12: Low confidence threshold at 10 is below the meaningful signal floor; raising to 12 will tighten the boundary between low/moderate confidence tiers, reducing false-positive high-confidence predictions on sparse archetype-surface pairs that drive calibration error.

decay.pressure_win_rate.half_life → 6: Pressure win-rate decay is completely untested; aggressive reduction from 12mo to 6mo will weight recent surface-specific pressure performance more heavily, directly targeting high-variance tactical adaptation where baseline model persists with stale historical patterns and contributes to calibration error concentration.

cluster.serve_weight → 1.5: Serve weight in clustering is untested; increasing from 1.0 to 1.5 will amplify serve-pattern dominance in archetype formation, directly addressing the baseline model's persistent miscalibration on serve-dependent predictions where recent neutral experiments in decay/confidence categories suggest serve architecture is the bottleneck.

window.serve_plus1 → 1: Serve+1 window is completely untested; reducing from 2 to 1 will tighten the immediate post-serve context window, forcing the model to rely more heavily on serve direction decay patterns rather than diluting signal with broader rally noise, directly addressing the persistent calibration error on serve-dependent predictions.

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 18: Serve direction decay is untested; aggressive reduction from 36mo to 18mo will weight recent surface-specific serve placement patterns more heavily, directly targeting the persistent calibration error on serve-dependent predictions where all recent neutral experiments suggest the bottleneck.

cluster.rally_weight → 1.5: Rally pattern weighting in clustering is untested; increasing from 1.0 to 1.5 will amplify rally-structure discrimination to capture point-construction sequences on hard courts where baseline rally patterns are more predictive than serve-dominated archetypes, targeting the calibration plateau after 24 consecutive neutral/revert experiments across decay, window, and confidence.

cluster.k → 5: Cluster count is completely untested; reducing from 7 to 5 will merge over-fragmented archetypes and reduce sparse-sample miscalibration on hard courts where archetype diversity may be artificially inflated.

decay.serve_direction.half_life → 18: Serve direction is the only decay parameter untested; aggressive reduction from 36mo to 18mo will weight recent serving patterns more heavily to capture tactical serve placement adaptation on hard courts where first-serve direction against specific opponents shifts rapidly within matches.

decay.shot_type_mix.half_life → 12: Shot-type mix decay is the only untested decay parameter; aggressive reduction from 24mo to 12mo will weight recent forehand/backhand/slice distributions more heavily to capture tactical adaptation on hard courts where shot selection variance within pressure sequences is rapid and predictive.

cluster.serve_weight → 1.5: Serve weighting in clustering is completely untested; increasing from 1.0 to 1.5 will amplify serve-pattern discrimination to balance against the recent rally_weight increase, capturing serve-dominated archetype structure on hard courts where first-serve accuracy and serve-direction consistency are primary point-construction drivers.
